sn46.29:1.1: “Monks, I don’t envision any one other quality that, when thus developed & pursued, leads to the abandoning of things conducive to the fetters, aside from the seven factors for awakening.
sn46.29:2.1: “There is the case where a monk develops mindfulness as a factor for awakening dependent on seclusion, dependent on dispassion, dependent on cessation, resulting in letting go. He develops analysis of qualities as a factor for awakening… persistence as a factor for awakening… rapture as a factor for awakening… calm as a factor for awakening… concentration as a factor for awakening…
sn46.29:2.2: equanimity as a factor for awakening dependent on seclusion, dependent on dispassion, dependent on cessation, resulting in letting go.
sn46.29:2.3: “When thus developed & pursued, the seven factors for awakening lead to the abandoning of things conducive to the fetters.
sn46.29:3.1: “And what are the things conducive to the fetters?
sn46.29:3.2: The eye is a thing conducive to the fetters.
sn46.29:3.3: It is here that these fetters, shackles, & graspings arise. “The ear… the nose…
sn46.29:3.6: the intellect is a thing conducive to the fetters.
sn46.29:3.7: It is here that these fetters, shackles, & graspings arise.
sn46.29:3.8: These are called the things conducive to the fetters.”
